market_overview.headline: Market showing mixed signals with indices holding bullish structure but underlying divergence emerging.
market_overview.detail: SPY and QQQ both remain Full Bull with spreads intact, but SPY showing bearish cross risk on both 8W/13W (0.77%) and 13W/21W (0.75%). BTC in Bear Rally mode suggests crypto weakness amid broader risk-on positioning.

momentum_themes.headline: Electronic Tech & Storage Leading Momentum
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology sector showing explosive momentum led by storage and semiconductor plays. AI infrastructure and data storage themes accelerating with massive spread scores indicating strong institutional flow.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Dominance vs Tech Services Weakness

strongest.note: Perfect bullish alignment with Iran war driving oil prices and energy infrastructure demand
Industrial manufacturing benefiting from reshoring trends and infrastructure investment
Power demand from AI data centers and grid modernization driving utility strength

weakest.note: Software and cloud services under pressure from valuation concerns and growth deceleration
Biotech and medical device stocks facing regulatory headwinds and pricing pressures
Banking sector mixed as rate environment uncertainty weighs on traditional lenders

sector_analysis.detail: Clear energy/commodities vs tech rotation underway with Iran war driving energy surge while growth tech faces multiple headwinds. Traditional defensive sectors like utilities finding new momentum from AI power demand.

items.text: SPY showing bearish cross risk on both 8W/13W and 13W/21W EMAs — watch for broad market deterioration
Technology services sector 68.8% bearish with major names like CRM, ADBE, NOW deeply oversold
Health technology carnage with 72.7% of stocks in bearish structure — avoid the falling knife
High oil prices and Iran war escalation could pressure consumer spending and economic growth

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Iran war tensions drove oil prices up nearly 3% despite UAE leaving OPEC, creating inflationary pressures as US gas prices near 4-year highs. Airlines raised fares but travelers keep booking, while Trump's approval hits new lows amid war concerns and rising cost-of-living issues.

items.text: Oil surged ~3% on Iran war disruption outweighing UAE's OPEC exit — energy stocks rallied
US gas prices near 4-year highs from Iran conflict and refinery outages hitting consumer spending
Airlines hiking fares but demand remains strong — travel sector resilience despite higher costs
Trump approval sinks to new low as Iran war drives cost-of-living concerns higher
GM reports Iran war causing cost increases but premium vehicle demand holding up
